original_hypothesis.title: Genesis onGenesisSuccess Repeatable Call Overwrites User Claimable Agent Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Genesis.onGenesisSuccess can be called multiple times after the first launch because there is no 'isSucceeded' terminal flag. The only guard (refundUserCountForFailed == 0) remains true after success. claimableAgentTokens uses assignment (=) not addition (+=), so a second call overwrites unclaimed allocations. The OPERATION_ROLE operator could redirect all unclaimed tokens by calling with different distribution arrays.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The lack of terminal success state combined with overwrite semantics creates a critical vulnerability exploitable by the OPERATION_ROLE holder (backend ops wallet).

proof_of_signal.pattern: claimableAgentTokens[distributeAgentTokenUserAddresses[i]] = distributeAgentTokenUserAmounts[i] — assignment overwrites, no terminal flag prevents re-call

preconditions: Genesis has completed first successful onGenesisSuccess call
Users have not yet claimed all agent tokens
OPERATION_ROLE holder calls onGenesisSuccess again with different distribution

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No isSucceeded flag; no guard against re-entry post-success; = vs += on claimable
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After first success until all users claim

operational_questions: Is the OPERATION_ROLE wallet a multisig?
What is the typical delay between distribution and user claims?
Can a isSucceeded flag be added retroactively?
validation_requirements: Call onGenesisSuccess twice with different distributions
Verify second call overwrites first call's claimable amounts
Confirm users who hadn't claimed lose their allocation

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: OPERATION_ROLE calls onGenesisSuccess with legitimate distribution
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Wait for some users to not claim
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: OPERATION_ROLE calls onGenesisSuccess again with attacker-controlled addresses in distribution
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attacker claims redirected tokens; original users' claims zeroed or reduced

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Complete control over genesis token distribution
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: User funds stolen; trust in genesis mechanism destroyed

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: After first onGenesisSuccess, before user claims

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Multiple onGenesisSuccess calls for same genesis ID

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
